examples.title: Basic abort() Example
examples.description: Demonstrates basic usage of abort() function
examples.explanation: This program registers a cleanup function using atexit() and then calls abort(). The cleanup function is NOT called because abort() does not perform normal cleanup operations.

examples.title: exit() vs abort() Comparison
examples.description: Shows difference between exit() and abort() cleanup behavior
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates that exit() calls the atexit cleanup function while abort() does not. _Exit() also does not call cleanup but can return an exit status.

examples.title: SIGABRT Signal Handler
examples.explanation: Installing a signal handler for SIGABRT allows catching the signal, but since abort() terminates after the handler returns, the program still terminates.

examples.title: Using abort() for Error Handling
examples.description: Shows proper use of abort() for unrecoverable errors
examples.explanation: This demonstrates using abort() appropriately for fatal error conditions where normal error handling cannot continue.
